channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Karl-Anthony Towns scores 10 points in abbreviated preseason debut with New York Knicks
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Jalen Brunson thought twice about going 1-on-2 in transition, instead dribbled back outside and flipped the ball behind him to Karl-Anthony Towns, who made a 3-pointer with a defender in his face.
item.dc:creator: STEVE REED, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Jaguars take down Colts to snag 1st win of 2024 season
item.description: The Jacksonville Jaguars finally notched a victory on the 2024 season, as they took down the Indianapolis Colts on Trevor Lawrence's 25th birthday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Coco Gauff wins China Open final in straight sets, Sinner and Alcaraz advance in Shanghai
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Coco Gauff won her second title this season with a lopsided 6-1, 6-3 victory over Karolina Muchova in the final of the China Open on Sunday. Aged 20, the sixth-ranked U.S. player became the youngest China Open champion in 14 years.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Real Salt Lake clinches home playoff game with 1-0 victory
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (ABC4 Sports) - Real Salt Lake will be home for the playoffs, at least in the first round. RSL (15-7-11) extended its five-game unbeaten run with a 1-0 win away at San Jose, as Zac MacMath recorded his second straight clean sheet in goal.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Jets kicker's pre-game warmups interrupted by Vikings cheerleaders in hilarious scene
item.description: New York Jets kicker Greg Zuerlein had to wait for the right moment to kick pre-game field goals, as the Minnesota Vikings cheerleaders performed right in the middle of where he kicked.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Grand Junction softball pours it on Palisade for big league win
item.description: With a clear blue sky and beautiful Mt. Garfield as the backdrop at Palisade High School, it was the Grand Junction Tigers show on the softball field.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Caitlin Clark officially named WNBA Rookie of the Year
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11)— Caitlin Clark was officially named the WNBA Rookie of the Year on Thursday, and rightfully so. Clark averaged 19.2 points, 8.4 assists, and 5.7 rebounds per game. The Indiana Fever finished 20 and 20 on the year and made the playoffs for the first time in eight seasons.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: Indiana Fever star Caitlin Clark a near-unanimous choice as WNBA's Rookie of the Year
item.description: NEW YORK (AP/WGN) — Caitlin Clark has been named the WNBA Rookie of the Year in a near-unanimous vote, giving the Indiana Fever back-to-back winners after Aliyah Boston won the honor last season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Padres beat Braves to advance to NLDS
item.description: The Padres booked their ticket to the National League Division Series on Wednesday, defeating the Atlanta Braves 5-4 in Game 2 of the Wild Card Series at Petco Park.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5

item.title: Dodgers issue statement on Fernando Valenzuela's status
item.description: Dodgers broadcaster and former player Fernando Valenzuela has been hospitalized and will miss the playoffs this year, but the team is hopeful he will return for next season.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KTLA 5

item.title: Whittingham hopes to have Cam Rising back after bye week
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Coming off a disappointing 23-10 loss to Arizona last week, Utah's offensive problems were exposed, especially in the passing game.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: McMahon 'incriminated himself' in Netflix doc: Vince Russo
item.description: "I don't feel like there was an agenda or a narrative," Russo said exclusively on "Banfield." Unfortunately, Vince's issue is he incriminated himself.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Column: Lightning and thunder combo could bode well for Bears' offense
item.description: The Chicago Bears have developed a formidable 1-2 punch in running backs D'Andre Swift and Roschon Johnson, who have shown the ability to both smash and slice through defenses, but the success of the offense depends on the performance of the offensive line.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Baseball legend Pete Rose dies at 83
item.description: (KRON) -- Baseball legend Pete Rose died Monday at the age of 83. Rose's complicated legacy includes being MLB's all-time hits leader but being banned from the league for gambling on games. He died Monday morning in his Las Vegas home, TMZ said. The outlet did not reveal a cause of death.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: Giants fire Farhan Zaidi, Buster Posey named as replacement
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The San Francisco Giants have parted ways with President of Baseball Operations Farhan Zaidi and named Buster Posey as his replacement.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Basketball Hall of Famer Dikembe Mutombo dies at 58
item.description: Mutombo starred in the NBA for 18 seasons and is considered to be one of the greatest defensive players in the history of the sport.
item.dc:creator: Brayden Stamps, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The final day for the Oakland Athletics arrives ahead of next season's move away from the Bay
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — When manager Mark Kotsay removed his Oakland jersey for the final time Sunday, it was bound for the Baseball Hall of Fame. Brent Rooker? His final jersey with the name Oakland across the front will be preserved in a frame and displayed on his wall at home.
item.dc:creator: TIM BOOTH, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Fred Warner's pick-6 helps the 49ers snap a 2-game skid with a 30-13 win over the Patriots
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — Even after all the success the San Francisco 49ers had the past few seasons, a two-game skid and a string of injuries early in the season can raise some doubts for any team. Considered those answered for one day at least.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4
